programme.title: Murder in the Valleys
programme.desc: Documentary examining the biggest criminal investigation in Welsh history after three generations of one family were murdered in the village of Clydach, Swansea, in 1999.

programme.title: Murder in the Valleys
programme.desc: The police must figure out how to investigate not one, but three of their own when it emerges the prime suspect is a very well-connected former officer.

programme.title: Tina
programme.desc: Born Anna Mae Bullock in 1939, Tina Turner has sold in excess of 100 million records and earned numerous plaudits including Grammy awards and induction into the Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame with her first husband, Ike Turner. She was the first woman to grace the cover of Rolling Stone magazine and dabbled in acting including a memorable performance opposite Mel Gibson in Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome.

programme.title: Look Away
programme.desc: Documentary hearing from women groomed in their early teenage years by rock stars, as well as exploring the lengths the music industry went to in order to protect its own.

programme.title: Albert Brooks: Defending My Life
programme.desc: A profile of the actor, comedian and film-maker, including interviews with Sharon Stone, Larry David, James L Brooks, Conan O'Brien, Sarah Silverman and Jonah Hill.

programme.title: Adam Hills: Foot Fault
programme.desc: The comedian and host of The Last Leg takes viewers behind the scenes of a global campaign to promote para-standing tennis and its potential as a future Grand Slam and Paralympic event. Adam meets and plays with former Grand Slam winners and uncovers the barriers faced by aspiring tennis players with disabilities who want to be included by their sport at the highest level.

programme.title: Discovering: John Mills
programme.desc: The life and career of the English actor, known for his roles in Hobson's Choice, Great Expectations, Ice Cold in Alex, and Ryan's Daughter, for which he won an Academy Award.

programme.title: Charlie Hustle & the Matter of Pete Rose
programme.desc: Documentary charting former baseball player and manager Pete Rose's career, including his rise to fame and his involvement in betting scandals that led to him being banned from the game. The .81-year-old Former Cincinnati Reds star recounts his unlikely rise and reignites his quest for reinstatement in Major League Baseball more than three decades after lifetime ban for gambling.

programme.title: Discovering: Michelle Yeoh
programme.desc: The life and career of the Malaysian actress, known for Crazy Rich Asians and Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon, and her Oscar-winning role in Everything Everywhere All at Once.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of Sidney Lumet, who was Oscar-nominated for his direction of 12 Angry Men, Dog Day Afternoon, Network and The Verdict.

programme.title: Spector
programme.desc: How Phil Spector gained a sinister reputation as his success brought paranoia, then examining how he went to trial after Lana Clarkson's death was determined to be murder.

programme.title: The Yogurt Shop Murders
programme.desc: Trauma continues to haunt the community as family members search for healing, resolution and ways to hold on to the memories of the girls.

programme.title: The Eighties
programme.desc: A look at the evolution of TV throughout the 1980s, from VCRs and remote controls to family sitcoms and prime-time soaps.

programme.title: Quant
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the life of fashion designer Mary Quant, with contributions from Kate Moss, Vivienne Westwood, Edward Enninful, Dave Davies, Charlotte Tilbury, Jasper Conran and Zandra Rhodes.

programme.title: My Icon: Pam Cookey
programme.desc: The former England netball captain discusses the individuals that have inspired her during her life and career.

programme.title: Billy Joel: And So It Goes
programme.desc: Part one of two. A look at the singer-songwriter's life and career, featuring interviews, performances, home movies and personal photographs.

programme.title: Land of Primates
programme.desc: From monkeys to lemurs and apes to human beings, primates are unique mammals who have taken an extraordinary evolutionary leap. See how they put their brains to work. Millions of years ago, animals were stranded on the shores of Madagascar, leading to remarkable adaptations. A case in point - the ring-tailed lemur.

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: A powerful pride of 20 lions, led by three strong males, finds itself facing an uncertain future when it ends up with only one cub.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: A journey through the Mekong basin, one of the world's richest areas of biodiversity, with 20,000 plant species, 430 mammals, 1200 birds and 800 reptiles and amphibians.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Dorset's Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre continues to save countless primates from lives of cruelty, abuse and neglect. Series premiere.

programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: Cocos Island, a gem among Costa Rica's national parks. Beyond its pristine rainforest lies a marine wilderness filled with life, from sharks to sea turtles.

programme.title: Arabian Inferno
programme.desc: The bone dry deserts make the Arabian landscape almost inhospitable. But for three months of the year, rainfall turns a remote region into a lush paradise.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: Chile's Atacama Desert, which stretches for 600 miles and is the world's driest non-polar region. From menacing volcanoes to resplendent flamingos, it is truly unique.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Capuchin Louis makes the most out of being let outside for the first time as he plays a game of hide and sneak with Donna. Slow loris Bruce is given Botox.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison receives footage of a pet Tamarin monkey in need of a home, Bruce the loris gets a check-up and golden-cheeked gibbons Kim and Tien get cosy.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders return to Paris, Texas, to build a one-of-a-kind timber frame wedding chapel; the guys get a motocross demo from a professional rider; they visit a log chapel they helped build in Virginia to celebrate its anniversary.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark Bowe and the Barnwood Builders crew build a beautiful, two-story log cabin addition for an old brick home in the rolling hills outside Lexington, Va.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The guys head to Canaan Valley, W.Va., for the largest commercial project they've ever built; they work with massive timbers and make an intricate post and beam structure; Mark meets the team who will make this place a wellness center and spa.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders return to Canaan Valley, WV to finish the biggest commercial structure they've ever built, a two-story, post and beam wellness center with heavy duty steel brackets and intricate scissor trusses inspired by a local church.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Using a mix of old school techniques and modern day tools, Mark and the guys bring every log back to life in an 1800s log cabin; Sherman visits a log home addition in Virginia and Katherine checks in on the progress at Barnwood Village.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team renovate a camp situated on two ponds; with winter right around the corner, Chase knows they need to hustle in order to complete the renovation in time for the homeowner's Thanksgiving get-together.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team tackle a dilapidated island cabin with major rot issues; with a budget of $30,000 and six weeks to complete the project, the team must turn it into a secluded island retreat while maintaining all of the original rustic charm.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Ashley Morrill and her team of builders find themselves rebuilding an old oyster shack for a working oyster farm in Edgecomb, Maine; forced into immediate action, the team starts its preliminary work with snow still on the ground.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team take on a dated and dysfunctional cabin in Belgrade Lakes; with a budget of $40,000 and six weeks to finish the project, the team strives to turn this Long Pond cabin into the perfect family retreat.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: In Oakland, Maine, Chase and his crew work to save Eric's old family fishing cabin; Eric hopes that the renovation will give the cabin many more years of life so he can share his childhood memories with his own children.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team hike through the woods of Dedham, Maine, to restore a dilapidated family camp that was once the old schoolhouse; from the swarming bugs to the relentless rain, the team learns nothing will come easy at the old schoolhouse.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his brother-in-law, Ryan, are inspired by a visit to the Allagash Brewing Company where their client works; they brew up some ideas to redesign the cabin while using items from the brewery as well as custom pieces to transform it.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team renovate a 130-year-old cabin located on an island; with a budget of $40,000 and a 6-week timeframe, Chase must overcome the challenges of building on an island to make sure the cabin is around for another 100 years.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the team seek to transform a family's 1950s lobster shack into a cabin that will create a lifetime of memories for generations to come; they re-imagine the cabin's interior layout, preserve a mural and add an exterior widow's walk.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A couple calls on Chase and his team to help save their mountain camp which was leveled by a tornado; with an emphasis on outdoor entertaining, the team adds an open second floor while racing to wrap it up before the first snow falls.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Wheeler family enlists the Cabin Masters to renovate their old cabin to fit their growing kids.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The McQuaids want to build a cabin for their family's matriarch across from their own cabin on David Pond; the build becomes a blank slate for the Cabin Masters, who work to design a camp that can work as her home away from home for years to come.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: David and Bishop, owners of an uninhabitable cabin, ask Chase and the crew to get fun and funky with a new build with a roofline that faces the water, lots of windows to enjoy the view, and enough space to host their beloved gatherings.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: After nearly one hundred years in operation, some of the YMCA Camp of Maine's buildings need renovations, and the wish list includes new bathrooms, a bunkhouse for the cooks, a chicken coop for the camp's hens and a covered outdoor grill area.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: With the help of a local historian and a Revolutionary War expert, the team saves a legendary fort dating back to 1775 in Lake Lure, N.C.; Mark and Graham visit the Oconaluftee Cherokee Village where craftsmen keep the story of their ancestors alive.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Ryan builds a rack to display antique mantle beams; Katherine and Arlene from the Barnwood Living office talk to Mark about ways to reuse and sell scrap wood in the showroom; Teeshawn uses extra inventory from the yard to cut shakes.
